author: p-durandin
title: JS API E2E test cleanup fails - Permission denied removing Electron DLLs on Windows
signature: rm cannot remove electron.exe Permission denied|Flaky Test
root_cause: The E2E test 'after all' cleanup hook runs 'rm -rf demo-electron-app-project' on Windows after the Electron app test completes. Windows file locking prevents deletion of electron.exe and DLL files (d3dcompiler_47.dll, libEGL.dll, libGLESv2.dll) because the Electron process may still hold handles. The actual tests passed (2/2), only the cleanup step failed. This is a transient infrastructure issue unrelated to the PR changes (GPU Level Zero fix).
reproduction_steps: Run the JS API E2E test on a Windows runner: cd src/bindings/js/node && npm run test:e2e. The Electron process may hold file locks causing the cleanup to fail.
recommended_actions: Fix the cleanup hook to use Windows-safe deletion (e.g., rimraf or retry logic) instead of bare 'rm -rf'
Kill the Electron process before running cleanup in the test teardown
prevention_strategies: Use a cross-platform cleanup utility (rimraf) in the E2E test teardown that handles Windows file locks, or add retry logic with process termination before deletion.
historical_context: First occurrence of this specific pattern in the database.
